_comment: Chrome Web Store listing metadata — submit via https://chrome.google.com/webstore/developer/dashboard
name: EnigmAgent — Local AI Secret Vault
summary: Secure local vault for AI agents: resolve API keys as {{PLACEHOLDER}} references. Zero cloud exposure.
description:
EnigmAgent is a local-first secret vault for AI agents, developers, and power users.

Instead of hardcoding API keys and passwords in prompts, scripts, or browser automation — store them encrypted on your machine and reference them as {{PLACEHOLDER}} symbols. EnigmAgent resolves them at the moment they are needed, using AES-256-GCM encryption.

✅ WHAT IT DOES
• Stores API keys, tokens, passwords, and private documents in a local AES-256-GCM encrypted vault
• Resolves {{PLACEHOLDER}} references in AI agent tool calls, browser automation, and scripts
• Integrates with LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, LlamaIndex, n8n, and 40+ AI frameworks
• Works offline — no cloud sync, no telemetry, no data leaves your machine

🔒 SECURITY MODEL
• AES-256-GCM encryption with Argon2id key derivation
• Domain binding: each secret is bound to a specific origin
• All vault operations happen locally on 127.0.0.1

🚀 QUICK START
1. Install the EnigmAgent MCP server: npm install -g enigmagent-mcp
2. Start the vault: enigmagent-mcp --mode rest --port 3737
3. Add a secret: enigmagent add GITHUB_TOKEN @localhost ghp_...
4. Use in any AI agent: {{GITHUB_TOKEN}}

For documentation and integrations: https://enigmagent.com

permissions_justification.storage: Store vault unlock state and user preferences locally
permissions_justification.nativeMessaging: Communicate with the local EnigmAgent MCP server for secret resolution
permissions_justification.activeTab: Resolve placeholders in the current page context

single_purpose: Securely store and resolve credential placeholders for AI agents and browser automation
